systemOverview.purpose: Utterd is a macOS menu bar daemon that automatically detects new voice memos synced via iCloud, transcribes them on-device, classifies them into the appropriate Apple Notes folder using a language model, and creates the note — all without user intervention.

systemContext.name: iCloud Voice Memos sync directory
systemContext.relationship: The source of new audio files, synced from iPhone recordings

systemContext.name: Local language model
systemContext.relationship: Used for classifying transcribed text into the appropriate Notes folder

systemContext.name: Apple Notes
systemContext.relationship: The destination for transcribed and classified memos, accessed via AppleScript or the Notes framework

components.name: File System Watcher
components.responsibility: Monitors the iCloud Voice Memos sync directory for new audio files using FSEvents or DispatchSource. Responsible for detecting when a file is fully synced (not just partially downloaded).

components.name: Transcription Engine
components.responsibility: Wraps the macOS 26 on-device speech recognition API. Audio file in, text out. Reports transcription confidence and errors.

components.name: Classifier
components.responsibility: Takes transcribed text and the user's Apple Notes folder structure, sends them to a local LLM, returns a folder name. Implements a confidence threshold — low-confidence results route to a designated inbox folder.

components.name: Notes Writer
components.responsibility: Creates a formatted note in Apple Notes in the specified folder. Uses AppleScript or the Notes framework. Handles formatting, timestamps, and source memo references.

components.name: Pipeline Orchestrator
components.responsibility: Coordinates the end-to-end flow from detection through note creation. Manages sequencing, error handling, and retry logic. Owns the lifecycle of each memo as it moves through the pipeline.

components.name: State Tracker
components.responsibility: Persists processing state for each memo: detected, transcribing, transcribed, classifying, classified, writing, complete, or failed. Prevents reprocessing and enables retry of failed steps.

components.name: Menu Bar UI
components.responsibility: Displays daemon status (idle, processing, error), surfaces recent activity and errors, provides minimal controls (pause, resume, open log).

components.name: Configuration
components.responsibility: Watched directory path, LLM settings, confidence threshold, inbox fallback folder name, logging preferences. Stored as a config file.

dataFlow: Watcher detects a new, fully synced audio file
Orchestrator picks it up and creates a state record (status: detected)
Transcription Engine processes the audio and returns text (status: transcribed)
Classifier receives text + folder list and returns a folder name (status: classified)
Notes Writer creates the note in Apple Notes (status: complete)
State Tracker records the final status
systemOverview.dataFlowFailure: If any step fails, the orchestrator marks the memo as failed with the failure stage and reason, and queues it for retry on the next processing cycle.

keyDecisions.title: Language and runtime: Swift
keyDecisions.rationale: macOS-specific APIs (Speech framework, FSEvents, AppleScript bridging, menu bar UI) are all native to Swift. There is no practical benefit to using another language for this project.

keyDecisions.title: Processing model: Synchronous, single-threaded pipeline
keyDecisions.rationale: The expected volume is 3-5 memos per day. A synchronous pipeline processing one memo at a time is simpler to build, debug, and reason about. Async/concurrent processing adds complexity that is not justified at this scale.

keyDecisions.title: State persistence: SQLite
keyDecisions.rationale: A single-file SQLite database provides reliable persistence, queryability for the menu bar UI (recent activity, error history), and safe behavior if the daemon restarts mid-processing.

keyDecisions.title: Single process architecture
keyDecisions.rationale: All components run within a single macOS daemon process with well-separated modules. No IPC, no multi-process coordination. This keeps deployment trivial (one binary) and lifecycle management simple (launchd manages one process).

keyDecisions.title: No cloud dependencies
keyDecisions.rationale: All processing is on-device. The LLM runs locally. Transcription uses the on-device API. The only network activity is iCloud sync, which is managed by the OS and not by Utterd.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Under 100MB RAM while idle, minimal CPU when not actively processing
No noticeable impact during monitoring; transcription will use CPU but is infrequent
Must handle system sleep/wake, iCloud sync delays, and unexpected restarts without losing memos or creating duplicates
Launches automatically on login via launchd
Structured logging with rotation, sufficient for debugging pipeline failures without filling disk

epicDetails.title: Voice Memo Detection and Transcription

componentDetails.name: File System Watcher
componentDetails.syncChallenge: iCloud does not download files atomically. A file may appear as a placeholder or partial download before the audio data is fully available. Possible approaches: monitor for file size stability, check iCloud extended attributes, use NSFileCoordinator.
componentDetails.deduplication: Checks the State Tracker before submitting a file. File identity is based on file path and content hash.
componentDetails.fileTypes: Voice Memos are stored as .m4a files. The watcher filters for this extension.

componentDetails.name: Transcription Engine
componentDetails.input: A file URL pointing to a .m4a audio file
componentDetails.output: A struct containing transcribed text, confidence score, audio duration, and any errors

unknowns: Does SFSpeechRecognizer have a maximum audio duration for on-device processing?
What is the performance profile — how long does a 5-minute memo take to transcribe?
Does on-device recognition work reliably in a daemon context (no foreground app)?
What audio formats are supported? Voice Memos uses .m4a (AAC) — is this supported directly or does it need conversion?

stateModel.schema:
CREATE TABLE memos (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    file_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    detected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    transcribed_at TIMESTAMP,
    transcription_text TEXT,
    transcription_confidence REAL,
    audio_duration_seconds REAL,
    error_message TEXT,
    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0
);

errorHandling.transient: Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff. State stays at the pre-failure status.
errorHandling.permanent: Mark as failed, log the error, do not retry. Surface in the menu bar UI (Epic 4).
errorHandling.daemonRestart: On startup, the orchestrator queries for memos in detected or transcribing status and reprocesses them. Safe because transcription is idempotent.

deferredToLaterEpics: Menu bar UI, daemon lifecycle, launchd configuration (Epic 4)
Additional state tracker columns for classification and note-writing status (Epics 2 and 3)
